nodes.context: Street gang based in Birmingham, England, active 1880s-1920s, engaged in robbery, violence, racketeering, illegal bookmaking, and gambling.
Rival street gang in Birmingham, fought Peaky Blinders for territory.
Larger gang led by Billy Kimber, overtook Peaky Blinders by 1920.
Gang that moved in on Birmingham Boys after Peaky Blinders withdrew, solidified political control over Central England in the 1930s.
Rival gang; a member was shot and killed by Peaky Blinder William Lacey in 1905.

edges.context: Peaky Blinders fought the Sloggers for territory in Birmingham and its surrounding districts.
Birmingham Boys, led by Billy Kimber, overtook Peaky Blinders by 1920 after violent backlash over racecourse expansion.
Peaky Blinder William Lacey shot and killed a Summer Hill gang member in September 1905.
After Peaky Blinders withdrew, the Sabini gang moved in on the Birmingham Boys and solidified political control over Central England in the 1930s.
